implementationArtifact.specBody:
---
complexity: low
---

# Feature: Add Footer

## Summary

Add a simple footer to the bottom of every page with copyright text.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Footer appears at the bottom of the page
- [ ] Footer contains copyright text with the current year and "BrewBoard"

## Technical Approach

Add a footer element to `src/app/page.tsx`. One line, no complex styling needed.

## Out of Scope

- Do NOT write tests
- Do NOT add documentation
- Do NOT refactor existing code

## Validation

```bash
echo "Feature Add Footer validated"
```

quality.summary: The implementation produces a broken file: it emits two `export default function Home()` declarations separated by a block of internal-monologue comments. This is a TypeScript compile error — the app will not build. The footer content and sticky-footer intent are correct, but nothing runs. The spec also said 'one line, no complex styling needed' and 'do NOT refactor existing code', both violated by the added flex-column wrapper. Internal reasoning prose left directly in production code is a serious code-quality failure.

requirements.notes: Footer content ('© 2026 BrewBoard') and sticky-bottom intent match the acceptance criteria, but 'Do NOT refactor existing code' is violated (added flex-col min-h-screen wrapper) and 'One line, no complex styling needed' is ignored. The broken file means the criteria cannot actually be verified at runtime.

correctness.notes: Critical bug: the file contains two `export default function Home()` declarations. TypeScript/JS forbids duplicate exports; the build will fail. The internal monologue block sitting between the two definitions is also stray dead code. The second definition (the flex-wrapper version) is likely the intended one, but as committed neither can compile.

minimality.notes: Spec said 'one line, no complex styling needed'. The patch adds a flex-column wrapper div with min-h-screen, flex-grow on main, a full commented reasoning dump (~20 lines), and a second complete function body. Change is 5–10× larger than the task required.

code_quality.notes: Leaving raw internal monologue ('// Let's check layout.tsx.', '// I will just add the footer...') directly in production source code is a serious quality failure. Duplicate function declaration compounds this. Naming and JSX structure in the intended implementation are fine, but the surrounding noise is disqualifying.

risk.notes: Duplicate default export breaks the build entirely — highest possible regression. All existing page functionality is unavailable until fixed. No tests were added (correct per spec), but the compile break means CI would catch this immediately in any project with type-checking enabled.
